title: Crude Membrane protein extraction from tissues
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9d102","text":"Prepare High salt buffer, Carbonate buffer,Wash buffer,Protease inhibitors","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bj04v","text":"Instruments you need: ULTRA-TURRAX Dispenser T10 basis S25 (IKA)(or other dispensers); Ultracentrifuge.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw 20–50 mg of tissue or 15CM dish cells in 1 mL of high salt buffer. High salt buffer: To impair of ionic bonds.2 M NaCl, 10 mM HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.4, and 1 mM EDTA. Store at 4°C.
Homogenize tissues using an IKA Ultra Turbax blender at maximum speed ( ∼25,000rpm) for 30 s.
Ultracentrifuge the suspension in Beckman MLA 130 at 100,000 g for 10 min. The tubes should be balanced to a difference less than 50 mg.

Discard the supernatant and homogenize pellets in 1 mL of carbonate buffer as in step 2 . Carbonate buffer: To allow efficient removal of soluble proteins1 M Na2CO3 and 1 mM EDTA, pH 11.3. Store at 4°C.
Incubate for 30 min with gentle mixing.

Collect the non-soluble material by centrifugation as in step 3 (If the content of integral membrane proteins in the purified membranes is not at least 30–40% of total identified proteins, steps 4–6 can be repeated two to three times).
Discard supernatant and resuspend pellets in wash buffer as in step 2 . Wash buffer: To melt non-integral membrane proteins.4 M urea, 100 mM NaCl, 10 mM HEPES-NaOH, pH 7.4, and 1 mM EDTA.
Collect the crude membranes by centrifugation as in step 3 .
creator: Vivian Liu
